q: In 1678, the Dutch physicist ___ introduced the wave theory of light.
Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront may be considered as a source of ___ ___.
The new position of a wavefront is the ___ of the wavelets.
If the propagating wave with a frequency $f$ is transmitted through the medium at a speed $v$, then the secondary wavelets will have the ___ frequency and speed.
Secondary wavelets spread out in all directions with the speed ___.
A ___ is defined as a surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all points in the wave that have the same phase.
On a particular wavefront, at a given moment of time, all particles of the medium are undergoing the ___ ___.
Wavefront is always ___ to the direction of propagation (or) rays.
There are two types of wavefront; ___ wavefront and ___ wavefront.
The ___ wavefront from a source transforms into plane wavefront when it is far away from the source.
Wave optics includes the ___ characteristics of light.
Physical optics is another term for ___ ___.
The study of interference and diffraction of light is dealt with ___ ___.
Huygens' principle explained how the light ___ from a light source.
The study of light for which the ray approximation of geometrical optics is not valid is dealt with ___ ___.
Secondary wavelets propagate with a frequency ___.
The direction of propagation of light is represented by ___.
A wavefront is a surface associated with a ___ ___.
The secondary wavelets spread out in ___ ___.
Huygens' wave theory was introduced in the year ___.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is known as the ___ ___.
The phase angle is the position of a point on a ___ ___ of a wavefront at an instant.
The phase angle is one of the important characteristics of the ___ ___.
If phase difference are $0, 2\pi, 4\pi, 6\pi, \dots$, these two positions are in ___.
If phase difference are $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots$, these two positions are ___ ___ of phase.
___ ___ indicates the distance between two points measured along the direction of propagation.
If path difference are $0, \lambda, 2\lambda, 3\lambda, \dots$, it is said in ___.
If path difference are $\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, \dots$, it is said ___ ___ of phase.
The relation between phase difference and path difference is expressed as $\Delta \phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} ___$.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, a ___ ___ emerges from the slit.
In Young's double slits experiment, each slit acts as a ___ ___ of circular waves.
Two waves are ___ if they are of same frequency, same amplitude and are in phase.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called ___.
___ ___ occurs at the centre of the screen because waves travel the same distance and are in phase.
A crest (or trough) of one wave arrives at the same time as a crest (or trough) of the other wave in ___ ___.
The bright fringe at the centre of the screen is called ___ ___ ___.
If the path difference is equal to an ___ ___ of wavelengths, the waves will interfere constructively.
If the path difference is equal to a ___ ___ of wavelengths, the waves will interfere destructively.
Constructive interference results in ___ fringes.
Destructive interference results in a ___ fringe.
The position of fringes is given by the distance from the central bright fringe, denoted ___.
Path difference $= m\lambda$ for $m = 0, 1, 2, 3, \dots$ corresponds to ___ fringes.
The variable $m$ is called the ___ of bright fringe.
The ___ ___ ($m = 0$) bright corresponds to the central bright line.
The condition for dark fringe at $P$ is when the path difference is a ___ of half wavelengths.
The distance of the $m^{th}$ dark fringe is $y = (m + \frac{1}{2}) ___$.
Distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the ___ ___.
Fringe width is given by the formula $\Delta y = ___$.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of light source.
In Young's double slit experiment, bright and dark fringes are formed ___.
Bright fringes have ___ ___ in Young's double slit experiment.
Intensity of dark fringe is ___ in the interference pattern.
___ light means that it has a single wavelength.
___ of light waves is defined as the modification in the distribution of light energy.
If waves emitted from two sources have the same frequency and constant phase difference, the sources are ___.
The path difference for constructive interference should be equal to an ___ ___ of the wavelength.
The path difference for destructive interference should be equal to ___ ___ ___ of half wavelength.
Young was able to explain the alternate bright and dark lines as ___ ___.
Light waves from the two sources $S_1$ and $S_2$ are initially ___ ___.
The value $d \sin \theta = m\lambda$ represents the ___ ___ for bright fringes.
___ is defined as the bending of waves around the corners of an obstacle (or) through an aperture.
The amount of bending depends on the wavelength of light relative to the ___ of the aperture.
If the aperture is much larger than the wavelength of light, the bending will be almost ___.
Light waves diffract around objects and create ___ and ___ fringes.
In ___ diffraction, the source of light and the screen are at a finite distance from the obstacle.
In ___ diffraction, there is an infinite distance between the source and the screen from the obstacle.
The diffraction pattern consists of a bright central portion called the ___ ___.
On either side of the central maximum, there are alternating ___ and ___ fringes.
Successive bright fringes become ___ ___ proceeding outward from the central maximum.
Secondary wavelets from the upper and lower halves of the slit reach point $P_0$ in the ___ ___ to produce a central maximum.
For the first minimum in single-slit diffraction, the condition is $a \sin \theta = ___$.
Generally for the $m^{th}$ minimum in single-slit diffraction, $a \sin \theta = ___$.
The position of $m^{th}$ minimum $y$ is the distance from the ___ ___.
The width of the central maximum is equal to twice the ___ of the first minimum.
The formula for the width of the central maximum is ___.
The width of the central maximum depends on the ___ of the slit.
A ___ slit causes a wider central maximum.
Diffraction occurs into the region of ___ ___ of the obstacle.
Diffraction fringes are created by ___ of diffracted light waves.
To get the first minimum, the path difference between two waves from the centre and top edge of the slit is ___.
A ___ ___ is an optical device which consists of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces.
A diffraction grating can ___ light into its wavelength components.
Diffraction grating is used for the purpose of resolving light into ___.
Diffraction grating is widely used as ___ ___ in spectrographic instruments.
Diffraction grating is a crucial tool in the study of ___ and ___ compositions.
The two types of grating are ___ grating and ___ grating.
A ___ grating is manufactured by carving glass with a sharp tool in a large number of parallel lines.
The number of lines (or slits) per unit length is called the ___ ___.
The condition for maximum intensity in a diffraction grating is $d \sin \theta = ___$.
In the grating equation, $d$ is the distance between the ___ ___.
If the number of slits per unit length is $N$, then $d$ is equal to ___.
There is no ___ of incident white light at the centre ($m = 0$) in a diffraction spectrum.
In the first order diffraction spectrum, ___ light diffracts at a greater angle than violet light.
A ___ ___ is an example of reflection gratings.
A ___ grating is a shiny surface having many fine grooves.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a ___ ___.
The ___ ___ is the reciprocal of the number of slits per unit length.
Diffraction grating resolve light into wavelength components to produce ___.
Red light has a ___ wavelength than violet light.
For maximum intensity to be formed at $P$, the path difference from adjacent slits must be an ___ ___ of wavelengths.
